requirement.title: Implement Transaction Rollback and Atomicity
requirement.description: Multi-step transaction processing must be atomic. If any step fails, all previous steps must be rolled back or compensated.
requirement.current_gap: No rollback mechanism exists. If database save fails after API validation succeeds, the system state becomes inconsistent.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All transactions must be ACID-compliant (Atomic, Consistent, Isolated, Durable)
Failed transactions must leave no partial state
Implement compensating transactions for external API calls
Add idempotency keys to prevent duplicate processing

implementation_guidance.step: Wrap all transaction steps in database transactions
Implement compensating API calls for rollback scenarios
Add unique transaction IDs for idempotency
Create transaction state machine (pending/processing/completed/failed/rolled_back)
Store rollback actions in transaction log

requirement.title: Implement Structured Logging and Alerting
requirement.description: Comprehensive logging infrastructure with structured logs, correlation IDs, and real-time alerting for critical failures.
requirement.current_gap: No logging exists at all. Impossible to debug production issues, monitor system health, or meet audit requirements.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Structured JSON logging for all operations
Log levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL
Correlation IDs trace requests across all components
Real-time alerts on ERROR and CRITICAL events
Audit trail for all financial transactions
PII/sensitive data properly masked in logs

implementation_guidance.step: Integrate logging library (e.g., structlog, python-json-logger)
Add correlation_id generation at pipeline entry point
Log at transaction boundaries: start, success, failure, rollback
Implement log aggregation (e.g., ELK stack, CloudWatch)
Configure alerting rules for critical failures
Create log retention policy per compliance requirements

requirement.title: Implement Comprehensive Input Validation
requirement.description: All inputs must be validated against schemas before processing. Reject malformed, missing, or out-of-bounds data with clear error messages.
requirement.current_gap: No validation exists. Direct dictionary access causes KeyError crashes. No type checking, range validation, or format verification.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Schema validation for all transaction inputs
Required field presence checking (account_id, amount, currency)
Type validation (amount is numeric, account_id is string)
Clear, actionable error messages for validation failures

implementation_guidance.step: Define Pydantic models or JSON schemas for transactions
Validate inputs at system boundaries
Define business rules: min/max amounts, allowed currencies
Implement custom validators for account ID formats
Validate API response structure before accessing fields
Return structured validation errors with field-level details

requirement.title: Implement Dead Letter Queue and Manual Recovery
requirement.description: Failed transactions must be preserved for manual review and recovery. System must support manual override and reprocessing.
requirement.current_gap: Failed transactions are lost forever. No recovery mechanism, no manual intervention capability, no audit trail.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Dead letter queue stores all failed transactions
Failed transactions include full context (inputs, errors, timestamps)
Manual review interface for failed transactions
Manual reprocessing capability with override options
Audit log of all manual interventions
Retention policy for failed transactions (e.g., 90 days)
Alerting when dead letter queue size exceeds threshold

implementation_guidance.step: Create failed_transactions table with full error context
Store original transaction data, error message, stack trace, timestamp
Create admin API/CLI for viewing and reprocessing failed transactions
Implement manual override flags (skip_validation, force_process)
Log all manual interventions with operator ID and reason
Add monitoring dashboard for dead letter queue metrics

requirement.title: Prevent Database Connection Leaks
requirement.description: Database connections must be properly managed with context managers or try-finally blocks to prevent resource leaks.
requirement.current_gap: Connections opened without guaranteed cleanup. If exception occurs before conn.close(), connection leaks.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All database connections use context managers (with statement)
No connection leaks under any failure scenario

implementation_guidance.step: Refactor all database access to use 'with sqlite3.connect() as conn'
Implement connection wrapper class with guaranteed cleanup
Add connection leak detection in tests
Configure connection timeouts
Add metrics for active/idle connection counts

requirement.title: Implement Transaction Reconciliation Process
requirement.description: Periodic reconciliation process to verify internal state matches external systems. Detect and alert on discrepancies.
requirement.current_gap: No reconciliation exists. Cannot verify correctness of processed transactions or detect silent failures.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Daily reconciliation job compares internal DB with external systems
Detects missing transactions, duplicate transactions, amount mismatches

implementation_guidance.step: Create reconciliation job that runs daily
Query external API for transaction history
Compare with internal database records
Generate discrepancy report
Alert on critical discrepancies (missing transactions, amount mismatches)
Create dashboard for reconciliation status

requirement.title: Implement Retry Logic with Exponential Backoff
requirement.description: All external API calls must implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures.
requirement.current_gap: API calls fail immediately without retry. Temporary network glitches cause permanent transaction failures.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Retry transient failures (network errors, timeouts, 5xx responses)
Jitter added to prevent thundering herd
Log each retry attempt with attempt number

implementation_guidance.step: Use retry library (e.g., tenacity, backoff)
Configure retry for connection errors, timeouts, HTTP 429/500/502/503/504
Do NOT retry on HTTP 400/401/403/404 (permanent failures)
Add jitter to backoff delays
Log warnings after each retry
Add metrics: retry_attempts_total, retry_success_rate

requirement.title: Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern
requirement.description: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures by stopping calls to failing services. Allows system to fail fast and recover.
requirement.current_gap: System continues making API calls even when service is down. Wastes resources and delays failure detection.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Circuit opens after N consecutive failures (e.g., 5)
Circuit closes after M consecutive successes in half-open state (e.g., 2)
Fast-fail during open state (no API calls attempted)
Per-service circuit breakers (account validation, exchange rates)

implementation_guidance.step: Use circuit breaker library (e.g., pybreaker, circuitbreaker)
Configure thresholds: failure_threshold=5, timeout=60s, success_threshold=2
Create separate circuit breakers for each external dependency
Implement fallback behavior when circuit is open
Log circuit state changes
Add metrics and dashboard for circuit breaker status

requirement.title: Implement Connection Pooling
requirement.description: Database and HTTP connections must be pooled and reused for efficiency and reliability.
requirement.current_gap: New connection created for every transaction. Inefficient and can exhaust resources under load.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Database connection pool with min/max size configuration
Connection pool metrics (active, idle, wait time)

implementation_guidance.step: For SQLite: Use connection pool library or singleton pattern
For HTTP: Create requests.Session() at client initialization
Configure pool size based on expected load
Implement connection validation before reuse
Add metrics for pool utilization
Configure timeout for acquiring connections from pool

requirement.title: Implement Graceful Degradation
requirement.description: System must continue operating in degraded mode when non-critical components fail. Partial success is acceptable.
requirement.current_gap: Single component failure causes complete pipeline failure. All-or-nothing approach.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Pipeline continues processing even if some transactions fail
Non-critical features can be disabled without stopping pipeline

implementation_guidance.step: Separate critical vs non-critical operations
Add try-except blocks at appropriate granularity
Return partial success results with clear status indicators
Implement fallback values (e.g., cached exchange rates)
Add degradation state tracking
Log and alert on degraded operation

requirement.title: Implement Anomaly Detection
requirement.description: Detect suspicious transactions, duplicates, and anomalous patterns to prevent fraud and errors.
requirement.current_gap: No anomaly detection. Fraudulent or erroneous transactions processed without scrutiny.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Detect duplicate transactions (same account + amount + time window)
Validate exchange rates are within expected ranges
Manual review workflow for flagged transactions

implementation_guidance.step: Define transaction amount thresholds per business rules
Implement duplicate detection using sliding time window
Validate exchange rates against historical data (e.g., +/- 5%)
Create flagged_transactions table for suspicious items
Implement review workflow with approve/reject actions
Track and tune detection rules to minimize false positives

requirement.title: Implement Exchange Rate Staleness Detection
requirement.description: Exchange rates must be fresh and within expected validity period. Reject stale rates.
requirement.current_gap: No timestamp validation. Could use hours or days-old exchange rates causing financial inaccuracy.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Reject rates older than threshold (e.g., 15 minutes)
Alert on inability to get fresh rates

implementation_guidance.step: Validate API response includes 'timestamp' or 'updated_at' field
Compare timestamp against current time
Reject if age exceeds threshold
Implement rate caching with TTL
Alert if unable to obtain fresh rates
Define fallback strategy (use cached rate, reject transaction, etc.)

requirement.title: Implement Boundary Condition Handling
requirement.description: Handle edge cases and boundary conditions properly: zero amounts, extremely large amounts, null values, etc.
requirement.current_gap: No boundary checking. Edge cases cause undefined behavior or incorrect processing.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Round financial amounts appropriately (2 decimal places)

implementation_guidance.step: Add validators: amount > 0, amount <= max_amount
Reject null/None/empty string values
Use Decimal type for financial calculations (not float)
Round all amounts to 2 decimal places
Define clear business rules for edge cases
Test all boundary conditions

requirement.title: Implement Rate Limiting and Backpressure
requirement.description: Control request rate to prevent overwhelming external services and protect system from overload.
requirement.current_gap: No rate limiting. Under high load, system can exhaust API rate limits and internal resources.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Rate limit outbound API calls (per service limits)
HTTP 429 response when rate limit exceeded

implementation_guidance.step: Implement token bucket or leaky bucket algorithm
Configure per-service rate limits
Add request queue with max size
Return HTTP 429 with Retry-After header
Add metrics: requests_rate, rate_limit_hits
Configure different limits for different priority levels

requirement.title: Implement Timeout Handling and SLA Enforcement
requirement.description: Proper timeout configuration with different values for different scenarios. Enforce SLA targets.
requirement.current_gap: Fixed 30-second timeout with no differentiation. No SLA tracking or enforcement.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Different timeouts for different operations (connect vs read)
SLA targets defined and monitored (e.g., p95 < 5s)

implementation_guidance.step: Configure connect_timeout (e.g., 5s) and read_timeout (e.g., 30s) separately
Use shorter timeouts for retry attempts
Implement overall transaction timeout using asyncio or threading
Define SLA targets per operation type
Track p50, p95, p99 latencies
Alert when SLA targets exceeded

requirement.title: Optimize Memory Usage and Implement Streaming
requirement.description: Process large batches in streaming fashion to prevent memory exhaustion.
requirement.current_gap: All transactions loaded into memory at once. Large batches can cause OOM errors.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Stream processing for large batches (process one at a time)
Memory usage stays bounded regardless of batch size

implementation_guidance.step: Implement generator pattern for transaction processing
Add pagination to _fetch_data_from_source
Process transactions in chunks
Add max_batch_size configuration
Monitor memory usage metrics
Implement memory pressure detection

requirement.title: Implement Comprehensive Monitoring and Metrics
requirement.description: Complete monitoring solution with metrics, dashboards, and SLO tracking.
requirement.current_gap: No metrics or monitoring. Cannot measure performance, detect degradation, or track SLAs.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Metrics for all key operations (latency, throughput, error rate)
SLO tracking and burn rate alerts

implementation_guidance.step: Integrate metrics library (e.g., Prometheus client, StatsD)
Instrument all operations with timing and counters
Export metrics in standard format
Create Grafana dashboards or equivalent
Define SLOs and implement burn rate alerting
Set up on-call runbooks

requirement.title: Improve Error Context and Categorization
requirement.description: Preserve full error context and categorize errors for better debugging and automated handling.
requirement.current_gap: Error details lost through string conversion. No categorization or structured error information.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Structured error objects with category, code, message, details
Error categories: ValidationError, NetworkError, DatabaseError, etc.

implementation_guidance.step: Define custom exception hierarchy
Include error category and code in exceptions
Preserve original exception as cause
Store full error context in database
Implement error pattern detection
Create error category dashboards

reliability.criterion: Automatic recovery from all transient failures
Graceful degradation under partial system failures
Load testing passed at 10x expected peak load

data_integrity.criterion: No data loss on system failures
Complete audit trail for all operations
Data consistency verified across all failure scenarios

safety.criterion: Input validation rejects all malformed data
Manual override capability for edge cases

operational.criterion: Documented runbooks for all failure scenarios
On-call rotation and escalation paths defined

reliability_tests.criterion: Retry logic tested with transient failures

load_tests.criterion: No memory leaks under sustained load
